prompt: List ALL the papers in this notebook with their: 1) Full title, 2) All authors, 3) Year published, 4) Key topic. Format as a bibliography. Include as many papers as possible.
response:
**Al-Riffai, M., & Nistor, I.** (2011). **Effects of Compaction and Slope Steepness on the Retreat of a Sand Bed under Supercritical Flow.** 
*Key Topic:* Dam breach evolution, supercritical flow, and mobile bed modeling scale effects [1].

**Arias, M. E., Cochrane, T. A., Caruso, B., Killeen, T., & Kummu, M.** (2011). **A landscape approach to assess impacts of hydrological changes to vegetation communities of the Tonle Sap Floodplain.** 
*Key Topic:* Hydrological alterations and their impacts on floodplain vegetation communities [2].

**Arias-Hidalgo, M., Villa-Cox, G., van Griensven, A., & Mynett, A. E.** (2011). **Filling missing data in streamflow series for supporting models.** 
*Key Topic:* Streamflow data interpolation and gap-filling techniques to support regional hydrological modeling [3].

**Azimi, A. H., Cai, J., Hall, N., Oshikawa, H., Zhu, D. Z., & Rajaratnam, N.** (2011). **Mound Formation by Slurry Jets on a Slope in a Water Pond.** 
*Key Topic:* Sediment deposition behavior and mound formation from continuous slurry jet injections [4].

**Aziz, K., Rahman, A., Fang, G., & Shrestha, S.** (2011). **Artificial Neural Networks Based Regional Flood Estimation Methods for Eastern Australia: Identification of Optimum Regions.** 
*Key Topic:* Regional flood frequency estimation using Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) [5].

**Batten, W. M. J., Weichbrodt, F., Muller, G. U., Hadler, J., Semlow, C., Hochbaum, M., Dimke, S., & Fröhle, P.** (2011). **Design and stability of a floating free stream energy converter.** 
*Key Topic:* Design, scaling, and stability of floating free-stream energy converters for low-head hydropower [6].

**Brasington, J., Rennie, C. D., Vericat, D., Williams, R., Goodsell, B., Hicks, M., & Batalla, R.** (2011). **Monitoring Braided River Morphodynamics with an Acoustic Doppler Current Profiler.** 
*Key Topic:* Acoustic Doppler Current Profiler (ADCP) application for tracking sediment transport and morphodynamics in braided rivers [7].

**Busuttil, D., Peirson, W., Lee, G., Onesemo, P., & Waite, C.** (2011). **Laboratory assessment of the performance of porous coverings in evaporation mitigation.** 
*Key Topic:* Performance of porous coverings to mitigate evaporation from water storages and farm dams [8].

**Calluaud, D., David, L., Pineau, G., Texier, A., & Larinier, M.** (2011). **Turbulence kinetic energy dissipation rate estimation from PIV velocity fields: Application to the study of the flow in vertical slot fishways.** 
*Key Topic:* Estimating turbulence kinetic energy dissipation in vertical slot fishways using Particle Image Velocimetry (PIV) [9].

**Chen, H., Tkalich, P., & Malanotte-Rizzoli, P.** (2011). **Data Analysis and Numerical Modeling of Sea Level Anomalies in the South China Sea.** 
*Key Topic:* Modeling and analyzing non-tidal sea level anomalies (SLA) in the South China Sea [10].

**Daniell, K. A., Coombes, P. J., & White, I.** (2011). **Multi-level governance and politics of innovation uptake in the water sector.** 
*Key Topic:* Political maneuvering, multi-level governance, and the uptake of innovations in regional water management [11].

**Di Silvio, G., Dall’Angelo, C., Zaggia, L., & Rapaglia, J.** (2011). **Waves produced by ship displacement on adjacent shoals and lateral basins of navigation canals.** 
*Key Topic:* Ship displacement wave dynamics and associated sediment resuspension in navigation canals [12].

**Ha, T., Lin, P., Jin, S., Seo, K.-H., & Cho, Y.-S.** (2011). **Solitary Wave Run-up around a Conical Island with the Internal Wave Maker.** 
*Key Topic:* 3D Navier-Stokes numerical simulation of solitary wave run-up dynamics around coastal structures [13].

**Haddad, K., Rahman, A., Kuczera, G., & Micevski, T.** (2011). **Regional Flood Frequency Analysis in New South Wales Using Bayesian GLS Regression: Comparison of Fixed Region and Region-of-influence Approaches.** 
*Key Topic:* Regional flood frequency analysis using Bayesian Generalised Least Squares (GLS) regression approaches [14].

**Hamaguchi, T., Sapkota, M., & Kojiri, T.** (2011). **Numerical Demonstration of Distributed Hydrological Modelling Performance Based on Super High Resolution GCM Outputs Using Geostatistical Approaches.** 
*Key Topic:* Bias correction of high-resolution GCM outputs and subsequent distributed hydrological simulations [15].

**Iervolino, M., Pontillo, M., Leopardi, A., & Greco, M.** (2011). **Numerical Simulation of NSF PIRE benchmark with a two-phase depth-integrated model.** 
*Key Topic:* Two-phase depth-integrated morphodynamical modeling for evaluating sediment-liquid flows [16].

**Jafari, A., Cartwright, N., Nielsen, P., & Callaghan, D.** (2011). **Stormy Wave Analysis Based on Field Observation on South-East Coasts of Queensland.** 
*Key Topic:* Analysis of storm wave parameters, wave setup, and storm surges using field observations [17].

**Jahra, F., Kawahara, Y., Hasegawa, F., & Yamamoto, T.** (2011). **Prediction of Momentum and Solute Transfer in a Vegetated Compound Channel.** 
*Key Topic:* 3D numerical simulation of momentum and solute transport dynamics across emergent floodplain vegetation [18].

**Kantoush, S. A., Sumi, T., Kojiri, T., Saber, M., Elshennawy, I., Awad, H., & Sefelnaser, A.** (2011). **JE-HydroNet: Modern Methodologies for the Management, Monitoring and Planning of Integrated Water Resources in the Nile Delta of Egypt.** 
*Key Topic:* International joint research methodologies for integrated water resources management and flash flood prevention in the Nile Delta [19].

**Kavetski, D., Evin, G., Clark, M. P., Thyer, M. A., Kuczera, G., Renard, B., Fenicia, F., & Tuteja, N.** (2011). **Battling hydrological monsters: Insights into numerical approximations, data uncertainty and structural errors.** 
*Key Topic:* Numerical artifacts, structural errors, and predictive uncertainty diagnostics in conceptual hydrological modeling [20].

**Krajewski, W. F., Weber, L. J., & Young, N. C.** (2011). **Iowa Flood Center: Resource for Critical Research and Applications.** 
*Key Topic:* Development of flood frequency estimation, inundation mapping, and public preparedness tools [21].

**Lee, H. S., Ding, F., Hendri, & Yamashita, T.** (2011). **Natural hazards education using Regional Environment Simulator.** 
*Key Topic:* Educational applications of the Regional Environment Simulator (RES) for natural hazard prevention and policy assessment [22].

**Liu, Y., & Pender, G.** (2011). **A New Fast Inundation Model Using Support Vector Regression and Modified Linear Interpolation.** 
*Key Topic:* High-speed modeling techniques for predicting flood inundation [23].

**Michioku, K., Miyamoto, H., Kanda, K., Ohchi, Y., Aga, K., Morioka, J., Uotani, T., Yoshida, K., & Yoshimura, S.** (2011). **H-ADCP Food Routing and Flow Modeling for Evaluation of Flow Conveyance Capacity in a Tree-Vegetated River Channel.** 
*Key Topic:* Hydrodynamic modeling and flow conveyance capacity evaluation in tree-vegetated river channels [24].

**Nistor, I., Palermo, D., Al-Faesly, T., & Cornett, A.** (2011). **Field Investigations and Experimental Modelling of the Tsunami-Induced Extreme Hydrodynamic Forces on Structures.** 
*Key Topic:* Experimental and numerical modeling of extreme hydrodynamic forces from tsunami bores impacting nearshore structures [25].

**Proust, S., Peltier, Y., Fernandes, J., Leal, J., Thollet, F., Lagouy, M., & Rivière, N.** (2011). **Effect of different inlet flow conditions on turbulence in a straight compound open channel.** 
*Key Topic:* Flow condition impacts and turbulence dynamics in straight compound open channels [26].

**Rehana, S., & Mujumdar, P. P.** (2011). **River Water Quality Response to Climate Change.** 
*Key Topic:* Climate change impacts on river water quality (specifically Dissolved Oxygen) and fuzzy waste load allocation modeling [27].

**Robinson, K. A., Bennett, J., Ling, F., & Willis, M. I.** (2011). **A Flexible Approach for Integrating Climate Change Projections with Changes in Human Water Use on River Systems.** 
*Key Topic:* Integrating climate change scenarios with human water-use data for flexible river system modeling [28].

**Saffarpour, S., Adams, R., Weatherley, A. J., Folkman, V., Grace, M. R., & Western, A. W.** (2011). **The Major Ion and Nutrient Behaviour of streams on a dairy Farm, Victoria, Australia.** 
*Key Topic:* Major ion concentrations and nutrient dynamics (nitrogen and phosphorus) in agricultural catchments [29].

**Salim, S., & Jayaratne, R.** (2011). **Studies on Hydrodynamic Erosion with Soil Protrusion Apparatus.** 
*Key Topic:* Hydrodynamic behavior of soil erosion and soil erodibility mapping using physical models [30].

**Sargison, J. E., Wallis, M., Lord, B., Walker, G., & Andrewartha, J.** (2011). **Wet feet in the ivory tower – a 50 year research partnership supporting hydropower development in Tasmania.** 
*Key Topic:* Academic-industry partnerships in hydropower, transient operations, and biofouling remediation [31].

**Smith, T. J., Piotrowski, J. A., Young, N. C., Schnoebelen, D. J., & Weber, L. J.** (2011). **Simulation of Spatial and Temporal Trends in Upper Mississippi River Physical Habitat.** 
*Key Topic:* 2D hydrodynamic simulation assessing spatial and temporal changes in river physical habitats [32].

**Souliotis, D., & Prinos, P.** (2011). **Numerical Simulation of Exchange Flows through Rigid, Emergent Vegetation.** 
*Key Topic:* Simulation of density currents and lock-exchange flows through emergent vegetation [33].

**St-Germain, P., Nistor, I., Palermo, D., & Townsend, R.** (2011). **SPH Modeling of Extreme Hydrodynamic Forces on Slender Structures.** 
*Key Topic:* Smoothed Particle Hydrodynamics (SPH) modeling of hydraulic bores and tsunami forces impacting slender structures [34].

**Xia, J. Q., Falconer, R. A., Lin, B. L., & Tan, G. M.** (2011). **Theoretical Estimation and Numerical modelling of annual energy output from a tidal barrage.** 
*Key Topic:* Hydrodynamic modeling and estimation of tidal barrage renewable energy outputs [35].
